text: your savings account paying 0.01% is not a savings account. it's a donation to the bank.
unpopular opinion: 'i can't afford it' is a complete sentence and saying it out loud is a flex.
the car payment to look rich costs more per month than the investment account to actually get rich. every time.
pov: you check your bank app 2 times a week and doordash 23 times. the math is mathing and it's not in your favor.
nobody tells you the first $1,000 is the hardest. after that it's the same habit on repeat.
63% of stock advice on social media is misleading. if they're selling a course, that number goes up.
your bank charges you $34 for having $0. sit with that.
compound interest doesn't care about your vibes. it cares that you started at 16 instead of 26.
tutoring pays 3x minimum wage and every teenager ignores it to try dropshipping. make it make sense.
'i'll start saving when i make more money' — said by everyone at every income level forever. the habit is the thing, not the amount.
loud budgeting update: 42% of gen z now says 'i'm broke' out loud instead of doom spending to hide it. we love progress.
your first paycheck being smaller than you calculated is the entire american financial literacy curriculum in one moment.
local scholarships get a fraction of the applicants national ones do. your counselor won't mention it. i just did.
spending is weather. investing is climate. stop checking the forecast every day and start changing yours.
a credit score isn't a measure of wealth. it's a measure of how profitable you are to lenders. play the game, don't get played.
the most important payment you'll make this week will get zero likes on venmo. do it anyway.
broke at 25 and rich at 40 are usually the same person. the difference is which year they started paying attention.
if a finance guru's whole personality is renting lambos, the product is you.
30-day challenge: every non-essential purchase gets a 24-hour wait. most people free up $300-600. that's a first investment, not a sacrifice.
banks pray you never learn the difference between 0.01% and 4%. it's the same insurance. it's 400x the interest. move your money.
pov: you split a $40 hoodie into 4 buy-now-pay-later payments and forgot the third one is due tomorrow. that's not a discount, it's a subscription to your own money.
nobody tells you that skipping your 401k match is turning down a guaranteed raise. your employer offers to double part of your paycheck and most people never say yes.
unpopular opinion: getting a raise and keeping your spending exactly the same is the only way the raise actually counts. otherwise inflation and a bigger apartment just ate it for you.

start a roth ira at 18 with $50 a month and you retire with over $100,000 more than someone who starts the same $50 a month at 28. that's the cost of waiting ten years, not the cost of not having money.
you 'treated yourself' four times this week and called each one a coincidence. your bank statement already diagnosed the pattern, it's just waiting for you to open the app.
